title: buxus sempervirens subsp. hyrcana
intro: Buxus sempervirens subsp. hyrcana, also known as the Persian or Iranian boxwood, is a subspecies of Buxus sempervirens, a small evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East and the Caucasus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, fever, and skin infections. The health benefits of Buxus sempervirens subsp. hyrcana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Buxus sempervirens subsp. hyrcana include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
